pr_title: perf: cache user permission sets to reduce DB calls
pr_description: Permission lookups were the top DB hotspot (avg 40ms, 15 calls/request). This PR adds an in-process LRU cache for `get_user_permissions()`. Cache entries expire after 5 minutes. Invalidation is triggered on role changes.
diff:
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/auth/permission_cache.py
@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+import time
+import threading
+from functools import lru_cache
+from db.models import Permission, UserRole, db
+
+_cache: dict[int, tuple[float, frozenset]] = {}  # user_id -> (expires_at, perms)
+_lock = threading.Lock()
+TTL = 300  # 5 minutes
+
+
+def get_permissions(user_id: int) -> frozenset[str]:
+    """Return the permission set for user_id, using cache if fresh."""
+    now = time.time()
+    # BUG: check outside lock — another thread could invalidate between check and use
+    if user_id in _cache:
+        expires_at, perms = _cache[user_id]  # BUG: read outside lock
+        if expires_at > now:
+            return perms  # stale data possible if invalidation raced with this read
+
+    with _lock:
+        # Double-checked locking — correct pattern but the first check above is unsynchronised
+        if user_id in _cache:
+            expires_at, perms = _cache[user_id]
+            if expires_at > now:
+                return perms
+        # Fetch from DB
+        roles = UserRole.query.filter_by(user_id=user_id).all()
+        perms = frozenset(
+            p.name
+            for role in roles
+            for p in Permission.query.filter_by(role_id=role.role_id).all()
+        )
+        _cache[user_id] = (now + TTL, perms)
+    return perms
+
+
+def invalidate(user_id: int) -> None:
+    """Remove cache entry for user_id after a role change."""
+    # BUG: no lock — dict.pop is not atomic in all implementations
+    _cache.pop(user_id, None)
+
+
+def invalidate_all() -> None:
+    """Clear the entire cache (e.g. after bulk permission update)."""
+    # BUG: replaces reference, threads holding old reference still use stale dict
+    global _cache
+    _cache = {}  # BUG: old _cache dict still used by threads mid-read
+
+
+--- a/src/auth/roles.py
+++ b/src/auth/roles.py
@@ -18,12 +18,16 @@ from auth.permission_cache import invalidate
 
 def assign_role(user_id: int, role_name: str) -> None:
     """Assign a role to a user and invalidate cache."""
     role = Role.query.filter_by(name=role_name).first_or_404()
     existing = UserRole.query.filter_by(user_id=user_id, role_id=role.id).first()
     if not existing:
         db.session.add(UserRole(user_id=user_id, role_id=role.id))
-        db.session.commit()
-        invalidate(user_id)
+        invalidate(user_id)   # BUG: invalidate before commit — cache is cleared,
+        db.session.commit()   # next read refills cache with OLD data from DB
+                              # because the commit hasn't landed yet
